ligand coordinates, metal coordination geometry, and bound-state contact geometry

e64_context_policy.context_derivation: V67 E64 uses sequence plus public non-coordinate metadata. Explicit transmembrane/topology-provider evidence may initialize membrane grammar; hydrophobicity-only and generic membrane mentions do not. Generic 'complex' wording is not oligomer evidence by itself.
reasons: public metadata indicates multiple copies or specific oligomer/assembly context
E64 treats generic membrane or hydrophobicity-only signal as insufficient topology evidence

prediction_sources.evidence_statement: Raw amino-acid sequence for the RCSB polymer entity.

RCSB title: HEPATITIS C VIRUS NS3 HELICASE DOMAIN COMPLEXED WITH SINGLE STRANDED SDNA. Entity description: PROTEIN (NS3 PROTEIN). Organism: Hepatitis C virus (isolate H). Polymer composition: protein/NA. Sequence-derived marks: sequence-derived membrane tendency high; no membrane topology without E64 topology evidence; sequence-derived low-complexity tendency high; sequence-derived hydrophobic closure tendency; public metadata indicates multiple polymer instances or molecule copies. Coordinates, native contacts, residue-residue distances, ligand geometry, and validation annotations are unopened before the prediction hash.

V67 E64 metadata context marks: assembly_context oligomer_context partner_copy_context. E64 uses explicit transmembrane/topology evidence and ignores generic complex wording as oligomer evidence. no membrane topology; hydrophobicity-alone or generic membrane signal is ambiguous. Coordinates, contacts, ligand geometry, native topology, and post-seal validation labels are blocked before sealing.
metadata_context_reasons: public metadata indicates multiple copies or specific oligomer/assembly context
E64 treats generic membrane or hydrophobicity-only signal as insufficient topology evidence
